{{Quote|Regal in disposition, confident in nature, strong, swift, and smart, tusk-cats have long-range eyesight and an acute sense of smell.|[[Lyrre T'enna]]'s notes on the Tusk-cat.|<ref name="Wildlife" />}}
The '''tusk cat''' was a fawn-colored [[Feline/Legends|feline]] predator found on the world of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] and its moon [[Rori/Legends|Rori]].

==Biology==
[[File:Tuskcats2-woswfg.jpg|thumb|left|240px|A tusk cat and its cub]]
Tusk cats were large [[Feline/Legends|feline]] predators with a large pair of distinct tusks growing from their lower jaws. This put them at morphological odds with many tusked animals which often featured tusks growing from the upper jaw. A second, less-noticeable pair of tusk-like fleshy appendages grew from their [[Bone/Legends|cheekbones]]. Tusk cats were covered in fur that could range from browns to grays to almost red, and gray ones had faint spotted patterns. They seemed to share some features with [[ungulate]]s, with swift galloping gaits and toes ending in hoof-like appendages. Some smaller subspecies featured more claw-like digits as well as variations on the number of tusk-like facial wattles. Their tails were long and muscular to balance them as they ran, ending in a leaf-shaped spur. They were known for keen eyesight and a sharp sense of smell. They appeared similar to the larger Naboo predator known as the [[Narglatch/Legends|narglatch]].

==Behavior==
{{Quote|Domesticated tusk-cats are naturally gentle and protective of Naboo children.|Lyrre T'enna's notes on the Tusk-cat.|<ref name="Wildlife" />}}
[[File:Tuskcat-woswfg.jpg|thumb|right|240px|A domesticated tusk cat and a Human child]]
Tusk cats were carnivorous and preyed upon smaller animals native to [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. Natural shepherds, they mated for life and instinctively formed small family groups, or prides, to guard herds of [[Shaak/Legends|shaak]] and [[Gualama/Legends|gualama]], killing one to eat every seven or eight days. Their tusks were used both as offensive hunting weapons and for defense.<ref name="Wildlife" />

Prides usually consisted of a mated pair and their offspring. Young males left the pride upon maturity and headed off to find mates of their own. Males found their female partners through a process of scenting.<ref name="Wildlife" />

Though they were aggressive predators, as cubs they could easily be domesticated and even ridden, but could also adapt back to the wild if released.<ref name="Wildlife" />

==History==
===Mounts===
Despite their predatory nature, tusk cats could be tamed by humans of Naboo for a variety of uses, notably as mounts. Domesticated tusk cats were said to be naturally protective and gentle towards Naboo human children. Human tusk cat riders were employed as shepherds for various livestock, including domesticated shaak and the royal gualama herd. Tusk cats also served as military patrol vehicles for the human [[Royal Naboo Security Forces/Legends|Naboo military]], recreational attractions, police work, and ceremonial beasts with Naboo human royalty. When outfitting a tusk cat mount, the traditional bridle was abandoned in favor of a harness attached to the tusks.<ref name="Wildlife" /> During the [[Victory Races of Naboo]], [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]] rode a [[Padmé Amidala's tusk cat|tusk cat]].<ref name="Yoda's Challenge">''[[Star Wars: Yoda's Challenge Activity Center]]''</ref> The tusks of dead tusk cats were typically carved into memorial cups and displayed by humans.<ref name="Wildlife" />
[[File:Tuskcatriders-woswfg.jpg|thumb|240px|Ceremonial tusk cats.]]
===Pets===
Tusk cats were among many creatures moved off-planet as pets following the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]] due to the growing demand for Naboo pets among [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]].

After the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Maldien]] conducted illegal and highly dangerous [[bioengineering]] experiments in a [[Illegal Lab (Corellia)|laboratory]] on [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]] to create very aggressive and intelligent creatures. This included engineered tusk cats.<ref name="SWG:CMCOtZ">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Coronet Murmurs: Clear Out the Zoo|Corellia}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Tusk cats appear both as wild creatures and as [[Tuskcat Trooper|mounts for the Naboo cavalry units]] in ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns]]'',<ref name="Battlegrounds: Campaigns">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns]]''</ref> replacing the [[Kaadu/Legends|kaadu]] in ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''.<ref name="Battlegrounds">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''</ref>
